creator: swills@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to mikael.urankar from comment #7)
We allow the user to pick which OpenSSL they use.

One of the supported options is the version from the base OS, which has things in /usr/ but doesn't have a .pc file. The configure script for kodi uses pkgconfig to find openssl, but since we don't have a .pc file for the one in base, that won't work if the user is using the version of OpenSSL from base.

Note tho that the configure script can also be told where to find OpenSSL via environment variables. So the solution at the moment is to add:

CONFIGURE_ENV=          OPENSSL_LIBS="-L${OPENSSLLIB}" OPENSSL_CFLAGS="-I${OPENSSLINC}"

to the port Makefile.

Perhaps at some point in the future the OpenSSL in base will have a .pc file and then all of the versions of OpenSSL that USES=ssl supports will be available via pkgconf.
